title: leucas decemdentata var. scaberula
intro: Leucas decemdentata var. scaberula, also known as Indian sage or wild sage, is a perennial plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and Sri Lanka. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective against various health issues. Leucas decemdentata var. scaberula has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, as well as to alleviate digestive issues like indigestion and diarrhea. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
